description: Fix a Python function that returns the wrong value
instructions:
There is a file called `calculator.py` that contains a function `calculate_average(numbers)`. The function has a bug — it divides by len(numbers) + 1 instead of len(numbers). Fix the bug so the function returns the correct average.

There is also a test file `test_calculator.py`. After fixing the bug, run the tests with `python -m pytest test_calculator.py -v` to verify your fix.

Make sure all tests pass before completing the task.

checks.name: visible: all tests pass
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "from calculator import calculate_average; assert calculate_average([1,2,3]) == 2.0; assert calculate_average([5]) == 5.0; assert calculate_average([]) == 0; assert calculate_average([-1,1]) == 0.0; print('all tests pass')"

checks.name: hidden: calculate_sum unchanged
checks.command: cd /home/user && python -c "from calculator import calculate_sum; assert calculate_sum([1,2,3]) == 6"
